manon-stand-light-punch.name: Standing Light Punch
manon-stand-light-punch.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; higher hitstun than other light normals allows a natural combo into 214LK/214MK; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

manon-stand-medium-punch.name: Standing Medium Punch
manon-stand-medium-punch.notes_sc: Moves forward during startup; easily hitconfirmable into TC follow-up (always a true blockstring even if delayed); high active frame count makes it good for oki; Punish Counter causes the follow-up Target Combo (5MP~MK) to change properties (causes hard knockdown instead of standing reset); strong Drive Rush check that can combo into 236LP on counter-hit for a medal

manon-stand-heavy-punch.name: Standing Heavy Punch
notes: Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent
manon-stand-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Very lenient hitconfirm window (always a true blockstring even if delayed); puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; Punish Counter causes the follow-up Target Combo (5HP~HP) to change properties (causes limited juggle state knockdown instead of standing reset); can be used in juggle routes to cancel into 5HP~HP or SA2 (e.g. after j.MP / 236P~K / corner 236KK / corner 236PP~K > 4HP)

manon-stand-light-kick.name: Standing Light Kick
manon-stand-light-kick.notes_sc: Longest ranged light normal (important for punishing -5 or -6 attacks); does not naturally combo into 214LK/214MK outside of Punish Counter situations

manon-stand-medium-kick.name: Standing Medium Kick
manon-stand-medium-kick.notes_sc: Front leg hurtbox is raised on frames 9-12, making it a good poke against low attacks; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

manon-stand-heavy-kick.name: Standing Heavy Kick
notes: Adds +17 frames of advantage on a standing opponent during a Punish Counter
Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent for a Punish Counter
manon-stand-heavy-kick.notes_sc: 3f extra recovery on block/whiff; incredibly long range poke that can combo into 214LK/214MK on Punish Counter from max range; Punish Counter puts airborne opponents into spinning free juggle state; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles; the slow startup and recovery makes it especially vulnerable to Drive Impact

manon-crouch-light-punch.name: Crouching Light Punch
manon-crouch-light-punch.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; true blockstring when chained into 5LP/2LP; cannot combo into 214LK/214MK, but gives more reliable 3-hit light confirm; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

manon-crouch-medium-punch.name: Crouching Medium Punch
notes: 2 frames of recovery added when the attack misses
manon-crouch-medium-punch.notes_sc: 4f extra recovery on whiff; Manon's primary combo starter

manon-crouch-heavy-punch.name: Crouching Heavy Punch
manon-crouch-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Very strong anti-air but cannot hit cross-up; the hurtbox on Manon's arm and head is invincible to airborne strikes, and her hurtbox remains in a crouching state for the entire animation allowing for late anti-airs to work

manon-crouch-light-kick.name: Crouching Light Kick
manon-crouch-light-kick.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; primary low combo starter; less pushback than other chainable light normals (allows tick throws and easier 3-light string confirms)

manon-crouch-medium-kick.name: Crouching Medium Kick
manon-crouch-medium-kick.notes_sc: Good range low poke that leads to combos and frame advantage when used out of Drive Rush; high pushback on hit and block; after Drive Rush, can link into 5MP at closer ranges

manon-crouch-heavy-kick.name: Crouching Heavy Kick
notes: Knockdown time extended when attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter

manon-jump-medium-punch.name: Jumping Medium Punch
notes: Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent
manon-jump-medium-punch.notes_sc: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; excellent horizontal air-to-air hitbox; lower body projectile invuln 8-22f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

manon-jump-heavy-punch.name: Jumping Heavy Punch
notes: Forces a slam knockdown when hitting a mid-air opponent
manon-jump-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

manon-jump-light-kick.name: Jumping Light Kick
manon-jump-light-kick.notes_sc: Cross-up; can be used as a fuzzy instant overhead (requires perfect safe jump timing)

manon-jump-medium-kick.name: Jumping Medium Kick
manon-jump-medium-kick.notes_sc: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

manon-jump-heavy-kick.name: Jumping Heavy Kick
manon-jump-heavy-kick.notes_sc: Incredible hitbox priority and downward reach (best option for most jump-ins); lower body projectile invuln 11-19f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

reverence.name: Révérence
notes: A quick attack that can be canceled into special moves
Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent
reverence.notes_sc: Strong punish starter and combo filler after Drive Rush routes; high active frame count makes it very strong as a meaty; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; lenient hitconfirm window, but late cancels into 236P, 214HK, or 214KK can fail to combo

tomoe-derriere.name: Tomoe Derrière
notes: A kick with long reach that launches opponents into the air
juggle state changes when attack lands as a Punish Counter on a grounded opponent
Forces a juggle state when the attack lands as a Punish Counter on a mid-air opponent

a-terre.name: À Terre
notes: A quick combination attack that pulls your opponent toward you
a-terre.notes_sc: Pulls opponent close to Manon for a strike/throw mixup on hit (but also easily punishable on block); always a true blockstring from 5MP; after Punish Counter 5MP, becomes a hard knockdown (HKD +32) that sets up strong oki anywhere on the screen

en-haut-1.name: En Haut (1)
notes: A two-hit series of attacks, the second of which cannot be blocked crouching
Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent

en-haut-2.name: En Haut (2)
notes: A two-hit series of attacks, the second of which cannot be blocked crouching
Adds a floor bounding effect during a mid-air hit

temps-lie.name: Temps Lié
notes: A two-hit combination attack that pulls your opponent toward you
2 frames of recovery added when the attack misses
temps-lie.notes_sc: Pulls opponent close to Manon for a strike/throw mixup on hit, but also easily punishable on block; 2f extra recovery on block; always a true blockstring even when delayed; easily hitconfirmed from 5HP; after Punish Counter 5HP, becomes a knockdown (KD +48 limited juggle state) that can combo into 236KK or SA2; this Punish version has extended range and inflicts damage scaling on the follow-up juggle; can be juggled into after 5HP in some combo routes (e.g. air-to-air j.MP, corner 236KK, 236P~K, or 236PP~K > 4HP)

allonge.name: Allongé
notes: A two-hit combination attack. Useful against jumping opponents
allonge.notes_sc: Causes spike knockdown after 2HP hits an airborne opponent; slightly better KD advantage when opponent is juggled high in the air

manege-dore-light.name: Manège Doré (Light)
notes: Attack changes according to medal level

Grab your opponent at close range, disrupt their balance, and toss them to the ground with a powerful throw. Landing this move will raise your medal level, and the move's effect will change depending on that level
Medal level increases by a single level on hit
Damage increases the higher the Medal level
Values in parentheses represent damage on Punish Counter

manege-dore-medium.name: Manège Doré (Medium)
notes: Attack changes according to medal level

Grab your opponent at close range, disrupt their balance, and toss them to the ground with a powerful throw. Landing this move will raise your medal level, and the move's effect will change depending on that level
Medal level increases by a single level on hit
Damage increases the higher the Medal level

Medal level increases by a single level on hit
Damage increases the higher the Medal level

Grab your opponent at close range, disrupt their balance, and toss them to the ground with a powerful throw. Landing this move will raise your medal level, and the move's effect will change depending on that level
Medal level increases by a single level on hit
Damage increases the higher the Medal level
Values in parentheses represent damage on Punish Counter
Medal level increases by a single level on hit
Damage increases the higher the Medal level

rond-point-light.name: Rond-point (Light)
notes: A swift, upwards kick performed while spinning. Useful in combos or, if done early, as an anti-air attack
The upper part of Manon’s body is invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 5 - 15
Only the first hit can be canceled into a Level 3 Super Art or Critical Art

invuln.strike: The upper part of Manon’s body is invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 5 - 15

rond-point-medium.name: Rond-point (Medium)
notes: A swift, upwards kick performed while spinning. Useful in combos or, if done early, as an anti-air attack
The upper part of Manon’s body is invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 7 - 19
Only the first hit can be canceled into a Level 3 Super Art or Critical Art

invuln.strike: The upper part of Manon’s body is invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 7 - 19

rond-point-heavy.name: Rond-point (Heavy)
notes: A swift, upwards kick performed while spinning. Useful in combos or, if done early, as an anti-air attack
The upper part of Manon’s body is invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 9 - 22
On hit, block or armored hit, can be canceled into a special move or Super Art from frames 44 - 46
Only the first hit can be canceled into a Level 3 Super Art or Critical Art

invuln.strike: The upper part of Manon’s body is invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 9 - 22

rond-point-od.name: Rond-point (OD)
notes: A swift, upwards kick performed while spinning. Useful in combos or, if done early, as an anti-air attack
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 4 - 16
On hit, block or armored hit, can be canceled into a special move or Super Art from frames 41 - 46

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 4 - 16

degage-light.name: Dégagé (Light)
notes: A powerful thrusting kick performed with all the grace of ballet. Useful for catching opponents by surprise and slipping through their guard

degage-heavy.name: Dégagé (Heavy)
notes: A powerful thrusting kick performed with all the grace of ballet. Useful for catching opponents by surprise and slipping through their guard

degage-od.name: Dégagé (OD)
notes: A powerful thrusting kick performed with all the grace of ballet. Useful for catching opponents by surprise and slipping through their guard
Low attack invincibility from frames 11 - 12, as well as throw invincibility

Spin toward your opponent and grab them before sending them flying. Landing this move will raise your medal level, and the move's effect will change depending on that level
Transition into feint by holding the button (total 33 frames)
Medal level increases by a single level on hit
Damage increases the higher the Medal level
Values in parentheses represent damage on Punish Counter

Damage increases the higher the Medal level
Values after the backslash represent damage on Punish Counter

notes: Spin toward your opponent and grab them before sending them flying. Landing this move will raise your medal level, and the move's effect will change depending on that level
Transition into feint by holding the button (total 34 frames)
Medal level increases by a single level on hit
Damage increases the higher the Medal level

notes: Attack changes according to medal level
Spin toward your opponent and grab them before sending them flying. Landing this move will raise your medal level, and the move's effect will change depending on that level

notes: Attack changes according to medal level
Spin toward your opponent and grab them before sending them flying. Landing this move will raise your medal level, and the move's effect will change depending on that level
Invincible against strikes from the knee and up from frames 3 - 26

grand-fouette.name: Grand Fouetté
notes: A kick that can be performed from the forward lunge in Renversé. Useful in combos or as a way of checking opponents from mid-range
grand-fouette.notes_sc: Startup of the initial spin depends on button strength; any invincibility from 236P is lost as soon as the kick follow-up starts (can slightly delay the follow-up to extend invincibility); cancel into SA3 is only useful if the kick is absorbed by armor; puts opponent into limited juggle state (free juggle state on Punish Counter); can be spaced to be safer in neutral

grand-fouette-od.name: Grand Fouetté (OD)
notes: A kick that can be performed from the forward lunge in Renversé. Useful in combos or as a way of checking opponents from mid-range

arabesque.name: Arabesque
notes: A kick performed while sliding forward along the ground that sends your opponent flying. This move is invincible on startup, making it useful as a way to turn the tables
Invincible against strikes and throws from frames 1 - 13
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter

invuln.strike_throw: Invincible against strikes and throws from frames 1 - 13

arabesque.notes_sc: Long range sliding low attack; switches sides with opponent on hit (except at max range); can be used in juggles; can trade vs. projectiles at certain ranges, allowing for a follow-up juggle

etoile.name: Étoile
notes: Launch your opponent into the air with a quick one-two combination of kicks, and follow up with a powerful leaping kick. This move executes quickly, making it useful as an anti-air attack or as a way to combo airborne opponents
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
etoile.notes_sc: If blocked, the last hit whiffs on crouching opponents; can be juggled into; extended KD time for opponent if they fall out before 3rd hit; full damage distribution: 450,400,500x2,950

pas-de-deux.name: Pas de Deux
notes: Attack as though performing a ballet dance and then smash your opponent into the ground with a devastating slam. Can be used to slip through an opponent's guard at close range and deal tons of damage all at once
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
Can only be canceled into standing heavy punch and Rond-point
Damage increases the higher the Medal level
pas-de-deux.notes_sc: Command throw that can be comboed into; opponent can jump on reaction after the Super freeze; damage at Medal Lv. 1-5: 4000/4100/4200/4400/4600; cannot hit opponents behind Manon; juggles into meterless 236P grant a restand that combos into SA3; cinematic time regenerates ~2.2 Drive bars for Manon; Range: 1.463 (2nd active frame 1.489)

pas-de-deux-ca.name: Pas de Deux (Critical Art)
notes: Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
Can only be canceled into standing heavy punch and Rond-point
Damage increases the higher the Medal level

ouchigari.name: Ouchi Gari
notes: Grapple with your opponent, slipping your leg between theirs and tossing them to the ground. Throws your opponent without switching places with them
Properties change to the following when the attack lands as a Punish Counter:
ouchigari.notes_sc: Can manually time a throw loop in the corner; Drive Rush also allows an auto-timed meaty throw that can be mixed up with 2MP or 4HP; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

uchimata.name: Uchi Mata
notes: Grapple with your opponent, slipping your leg between theirs and lifting them over you. Throws your opponent while switching places with them
Properties change to the following when the attack lands as a Punish Counter:
uchimata.notes_sc: Side switches; after corner side switch, can walk for a pseudo throw loop or Drive Rush for a manually timed strike/throw mixup; can get true throw oki vs. wide characters (1f timing vs. Marisa, 2f timing vs. Blanka/E.Honda/Zangief); midscreen, can chase down with DR~5HP but no real strike/throw mixup; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously
